purpose: A horizontal navigation trail that shows the user's location within a hierarchy and lets them jump back to any ancestor page. Common in deep object hierarchies and drill-down flows.
whenToUse: User navigated through 3+ levels of hierarchy
Multiple valid entry points exist (Search vs Browse)
The user needs to return to an ancestor without back-buttoning
The current page has meaningful context (Root > Category > Item)
whenNotToUse: Only one entry point exists (Home → Detail) — use back-button
The path is only 2 levels deep — Title with a Back button suffices
The hierarchy is a graph (many paths) — use a proper navigation menu
In modals or dialogs — they should not span hierarchical navigation
doRules: Show the full path from root to current
Make every ancestor a clickable Link
Make the current page the last item (non-clickable, in different style)
Truncate middle items with ellipsis when path is very long
dontRules: Do not make the current page clickable
Do not use a chevron other than the standard '>'
Do not truncate the root or current — always visible
Do not use inside dialogs, popovers, or table cells

layoutGuidance.placement: Just below the ShellBar, above the DynamicPageTitle

contentGuidance.labelLength: Each item ≤ 30 chars; middle items truncate with '…'
contentGuidance.contentRules: Item labels match the page titles they link to

responsiveBehavior.M: May collapse middle items to '…' with dropdown

requirements: Each link has descriptive text (not 'click here')
Keyboard: Tab moves between links; Enter navigates
